title: Chia Pudding
ingredients:
4 tbsp Chia Seeds
1/2 cup coconut milk (depending on the thickness of the milk you use, you may need a little more or a little less)
A few drops Vanilla Extract
Cinnamon (to taste)
Pomegranate Seeds
Roasted Shaved Coconut
preparation:
Preparation
Serves 2
Method
Stir Chia, vanilla and cinnamon into the Coconut Milk and place in the refrigerator for 20 mins. When ready to eat, add coconut shavings (raw or roasted) and pomegranate newbies - cut the pomegranate in half and score each segment. Pop the individual pomegranate seeds out with your fingers and sprinkle over your pudding.

title: Maple Cinnamon Granola
ingredients:
3 cups of rolled oats
2 cups mixed nuts and seeds (I used hazelnuts, almonds, walnuts, pumpkin seeds and sunflower seeds)
2 Tbsp coconut oil, melted
3 Tbsp maple syrup
1 tsp vanilla extract
1 tsp cinnamon
Pinch salt
preparation:
Method
Preheat oven to 150C/300F/Gas Mark 2.
In a large bowl mix together the oats and nuts/seeds. In a small bowl mix together the melted oil, syrup, vanilla, cinnamon and salt. Pour the wet ingredients over the dry ingredients and mix well.
Line a large baking sheet/tray with parchment paper and spread the granola out onto the tray. Bake for about 35 - 40 mins until golden, stirring every 10 - 15 mins. Leave to cool before storing in a container or serving with your choice of milk or yogurt and fresh fruit.

title: ALMOND BUTTER AND BERRY JELLY SHAKE
ingredients:
1 handful of frozen blueberries
2 tsp almond butter
1 tsp peanut butter
125ml Plenish Cashew M*lk
125ml Plenish Almond M*lk
0.5 frozen banana
preparation:
Preparation
Serves 1
Blend all ingredients together until smooth

title: Overnight Mango and Chia Seed Pudding
ingredients:
2 tbsp chia seeds
5 -6 tbsp yoghurt (dairy free)
3 tbsp plant milk
1/2 tsp vanilla extract
1 very ripe mango
a few almonds/pistachios
a small handful raspberries
a pinch of cardamom powder (optional)
preparation:
Preparation
Serves 1
Method
Whisk together the chia seeds, yoghurt, milk and vanilla until the chia seeds are distributed throughout the mixture. Cover and leave in the fridge overnight or at least 30 mins.
Add the raspberries and half the mango flesh to a blender or food processor and blend until smooth. Add a pinch of cardamom if desired.
Divide the chia mixture between 2 glasses or jars. Top with a layer of the raspberry mango puree.
Top with the remaining mango chopped into small chunks and some roughly chopped almonds/pistachios.
Try and leave it in the fridge overnight so it really thickens up. What an easy and delicious dessert you'll be eating tomorrow!

title: Carrot Cake Porridge
ingredients:
2 carrots (approx 150g)
80g raisins
800ml Alpro Almond No Sugars drink
1 tsp cinnamon
1 tsp vanilla essence
200g rolled oats
To serve: Handful of chopped nuts e.g. walnuts
preparation:
Preparation
Peel and finely grate the carrots.
Pour the Alpro Almond No Sugars drink into a saucepan, add the carrot, raisins, cinnamon and vanilla essence.
Simmer gently on a low heat for 5-10 minutes until the carrots are tender, giving it a stir every now and then.
Stir in the rolled oats and carry on cooking until the mixture has thickened up nicely - usually around 5 minutes.
Serve with chopped nuts.

title: Banana Breakfast Muffins
ingredients:
5.2 oz (150g, 80ml) melted coconut oil (can also use vegetable/sunflower or other mild oils)
1tsp vanilla extract
1 1/4 cup (5.7oz, 150g) wholewheat flour
3/4 cup (3.5 oz, 100g) self-raising flour
1tsp baking powder
? cup (3.5oz, 100g) granulated sugar
1/2 cup (100ml) of your preferred plant-based milk
2 flax "eggs" (2tbsp ground flaxseed + 6tbsp water)
4 medium banana's, mashed
1/4 cup of your favourite nuts, chopped (I used walnuts)
preparation:
Preparation
Pre-heat the oven to 350f / 185c / Gas Mark 5
Method
Mix up the two flax "eggs" by adding the ground flax to the water and set to one side.
Combine the wholewheat flour, self-raising flour, baking powder and granulated sugar in a bowl.
Mash the bananas and to this add your plant-based milk, flax "eggs", melted coconut oil and vanilla extract. Whisk until well combined and then add to the dry ingredients.
Fold in the chopped nuts and any other add-ins you desire and mix only so much to say combined. Don't over-mix as you will get tough/rubbery muffins.
Divide equally between 12 muffin cases and bake on the middle shelf for 20 minutes (or until a toothpick inserted into the centre comes out clean).
I topped mine with a little coconut sugar before baking to make a little sweet crust but this is entirely optional!

title: Granola
ingredients:
3 cups rolled oats
1 cup roasted pecans
1/3 cup sunflower seeds
1/3 cup pumpkin seeds
1/3 cup raisins
3 tablespoons cocoa powder
4 tablespoons coconut oil, melted
3 tablespoons maple syrup
1 pinch of sea salt
1/2 teaspoon cinnamon
preparation:
Method
In a bowl combine the oats, pecans, cocoa, sunflower seeds, pumpkin seeds and raisins.
Mix the coconut oil, maple syrup, cinnamon and sea salt.
Pour the coconut oil mixture over the oat mixture and stir to combine. Add the mixture to a baking sheet lined with parchment paper.
Bake in the oven to 180C/350F. Add the mixture to the baking sheet in the oven until it is golden brown and crispy.
Enjoy with almond yogurt and some fresh berries for an indulgent breakfast!

title: Garlic Gram Pancakes
ingredients:
1 cup or 92 g gram flour (AKA chickpea flour)
1/2 teaspoon garlic powder
1/2 teaspoon Himalayan salt
1 cup + 3 tablespoons water
1/2 teaspoon lemon juice
1/2 teaspoon baking powder
1 teaspoon + 1/2 teaspoon nutritional yeast (divided)
1/4 cup / 15 g diced onion
1.2 oz / 20 g diced red/yellow capsicum / bell pepper
1.6 oz / 45 g diced mushrooms
1/8 cup / 10 g fresh spinach
2 tablespoons oil
preparation:
Method
In a bowl whisk together the gram flour, garlic powder, salt and water. Add the lemon juice and then set aside for 10 -15 minutes. If you don't have time to rest the batter, it will still work but the pancakes won't turn out quite as fluffy. The batter will become a bit thicker after it has rested. In the meantime, chop the onion, mushrooms, capsicum / bell pepper and spinach.
Whisk the baking powder and 1 teaspoon of nutritional yeast into the batter. Heat a pan over medium to high heat. Ensure the pan is thoroughly hot before adding the oil. I use 1 teaspoon of oil per batch and spread it out with a silicone brush. Gently saute the onion, mushroom and capsicum / bell pepper until the vegetables are just cooked through. If your pan isn't thoroughly hot, the vegetables will release a lot of water. Add the spinach and saute until it has wilted.
Turn the heat down as low as you can. Make sure the vegetables are spread out over the pan and then spoon the batter in over the vegetables. I like to press the batter out gently with a spoon as I pour it in to make a 4.5 - 5 inch pancake.
The key is to only cook the pancake over low heat and not to rush it, otherwise it will taste raw in the centre. My ideal temperatures are 120 - 212 degrees Fahrenheit / 50 -100 degrees Celsius. Cook the pancake for 4 minutes and then carefully flip over. Drizzle a little more oil around the pancake if needed. Sprinkle 1/2 teaspoon of nutritional yeast over the top of the pancake.
Use a spatula to gently press down on the pancake and then cook for another 3 - 4 minutes. If the pancake still feels very soft in the centre, flip and cook again for another minute or two. If your pancake is difficult to flip in one go, just use your spatula to hold the pancake together and reshape it as best as you can. It doesn't affect the taste. Top with a little salsa or your favourite vegan sauce and serve!

title: Overnight Oats
ingredients:
4tbsp Natural Oat Yogurt (or your choice of yogurt)
4tbsp porridge oats
1tbsp chia seeds
2tbsp grated apple
4tbsp coconut milk
4 fresh raspberries
5 blueberries
preparation:
Method
Mix all ingredients together in a pot or jar and leave in the fridge overnight (with lid on). Eat cold the next day.

title: VEGAN BLUEBERRY BANANA OAT BREAD
ingredients:
1 1/4 cups (120g) old fashioned oats
1 1/2 cups (180g) spelt flour, all-purpose or light whole wheat flour
1/2 cup (56g) walnuts, chopped
1/3 cup (75g) sugar (pure cane or coconut)
2 teaspoons baking powder
1/2 teaspoon cinnamon
generous pinch of salt
2 - 3 ripe bananas, mashed (about 1 cup)
1/2 cup (118ml) unsweetened almond milk or vanilla almond milk
1/4 cup (56ml) olive oil, warmed coconut oil, or vegan butter/margarine
1 cup (125g) blueberries, fresh or frozen
preparation:
Instructions Preheat oven to 350 degrees F. Grease a 9 x 5 loaf pan lightly with oil.
Mix dry ingredients: In a medium to large mixing bowl, combine the oats, flour, walnuts, sugar, baking powder, cinnamon and salt, mix well.
Mix wet ingredients: In a small bowl, combine the mashed bananas, almond milk, and oil, mix well.
Combine and rest: Add the wet mixture to the dry ingredients, mix just until combined, fold in the blueberries. Let the batter rest for 5 minutes.
Bake: Pour batter into loaf pan. Bake on the middle rack for 40 - 45 minutes. If the top browns too quickly, cover with foil or parchment paper around the 30 minute mark. Ovens vary, mine usually takes 50 minutes. You can also do the toothpick test in the centre of the loaf, if it comes out clean it should be ready. Remove from oven and let cool for 10 min before slicing.
Store: Keep covered on the counter for up to 3 days or in the refrigerator up to 1 week. Keep in the freezer for up to 2 months. Thaw the wrapped loaves overnight in the refrigerator. Reheat in the microwave or a toaster oven with a door.

title: Mixed Berry Yogurt Smoothie
ingredients:
1 1/2 cups frozen berries mix (strawberries, raspberries, blueberries, and/or blackberries)
1 small banana, optional (but adds extra creaminess!)
1 cup unsweetened non-dairy yogurt (plain or vanilla)
1/2 cup unsweetened almond milk (plain or vanilla)
1 teaspoon vanilla extract or ground vanilla bean powder, optional
preparation:
Instructions Place ingredients into your blender, blend until smooth, stopping every now and then to scrape down the sides if needed. Add an extra splash of non-dairy milk or water as needed.
Serve as is in a glass with straw/spoon. Alternately, serve in a bowl making a smoothie bowl.
Either way you serve it, top with any one or more of these optional topping:
frozen/fresh berries
granola
coconut flakes
cocoa nibs
chia/hemp seeds
chopped almonds
a dollop of your favorite nut butter
Serves 1 large or 2 small smoothies

title: VEGAN BUTTERMILK BISCUITS & COUNTRY GRAVY
ingredients:
Biscuits
2 1/2 cups unbleached all-purpose flour
1 tablespoon baking powder
1/2 teaspoon baking soda
pinch of salt
4 rounded tablespoons of vegan butter (I used smart balance w/olive oil)
1 cup unsweetened cashew milk
juice of 1 lemon, about 1 tablespoon
Country Gravy
5 tablespoons olive oil or vegan butter
1/3 cup flour
2 teaspoons garlic powder
2 teaspoons poultry seasoning
1/2 teaspoon nutmeg
1 package sage sausage
3 cups unsweetened almond milk
sea salt and pepper to taste
preparation:
Instructions Gravy: In a medium sized pot heat oil over medium-high heat, add flour and stir for 2 - 3 minutes, until it turns light golden brown. Add the garlic powder, poultry seasoning and nutmeg, mix well, add in the sausage and stir another minute or so. Mixture will form a ball of sorts. Slowly add in the milk, stirring or whisking to remove any clumps. Continue heating over medium-low until thickened, stirring often. Add salt and pepper to taste.
Prepare biscuits: Preheat oven to 450 degrees F. In a large mixing bowl, combine the flour, baking powder, baking soda and salt. Cut in the butter using a pastry cutter or your fingers. Add the lemon juice to the milk and let sit a minute or two, this is your buttermilk. Pour buttermilk into the bowl with the flour mixture and mix until combined. Dough will be sticky.
Flour work surface and transfer biscuit dough to surface, sprinkle the top of dough with flour, knead about 5 times. Pat dough into a 5 inch rectangle. Using a pastry cutter, cut out 2 inch circles. Place each biscuit on ungreased baking sheet or large cast iron skillet. Place in oven and bake for 10 - 12 minutes, until golden on top and bottom.
Serve biscuits topped with gravy. Makes about 8 biscuits.

title: VEGAN PUMPKIN MUFFINS
ingredients:
2 cups flour (spelt, all-purpose, whole wheat, or 1-1 GF)
2/3 - 3/4 cup sugar (organic pure cane or coconut)
3 teaspoons cinnamon
1 teaspoon baking soda
1 teaspoon baking powder
pinch of salt
1 can (15oz) 100% pumpkin puree or 1 1/2 cups fresh pureed
1/3 cup unsweetened applesauce or oil (olive oil or room temp coconut oil)
1/3 cup unsweetened plant milk
1 - 2 teaspoons vanilla
chopped pepitas (pumpkin seeds), optional topping
preparation:
Instructions Preheat oven to 350 degrees F. Line 12 cup muffin tin with liners.
Mix: In a medium mixing bowl, mix together the flour, sugar, baking soda, baking powder, cinnamon and salt. Add the pumpkin puree, applesauce, vanilla and non-dairy milk, mix well (shown above). Don't overmix.
Fill muffin tin: Add batter by the large spoonful to lined 12 cup muffin tin, filling to the rim (shown above). Sprinkle with the optional chopped pepitas.
Bake: Place on the centre rake in the oven and bake for 20 - 25 minutes. To test for doneness, stick a toothpick in the centre and it should come out clean.
Cool: Let cool a few minutes before serving or storing.
Makes 12
Store: Keep covered on the counter for 2 - 3 days, refrigerator for up to 1 week. For longer storage, keep in the freezer for up to 2 - 3 months.

title: YUKON GOLD & SWEET POTATO HOME FRIES
ingredients:
2 tablespoons olive oil, divided
1 medium onion, diced
1 red bell pepper, diced
2 cups yukon gold potato, cubed
2 cups sweet potato, cubed
1/2 teaspoon garlic powder
1/2 teaspoon paprika
mineral salt & pepper to taste
toasted sesame seeds, optional
vegan sriracha mayo, optional
preparation:
Instructions In large skillet over medium heat, heat 1 tablespoon oil and cook onions and bell peppers for 3 - 4 minutes, stirring occasionally. Remove and set aside.
Heat remaining tablespoon oil in the skillet and add yukon gold and sweet potatoes to the pan, toss to coat. Cover with lid and let steam over medium-low heat for about 10 minutes, stirring every 3 minutes or so, keeping lid on each time. Potatoes are done when just fork tender.
Season: Add in the onion and bell peppers and spices. Continue to cook with lid askew for another 10 minutes, stirring every 2 to 3 minutes, allowing the potatoes to brown.
Serve with sesame seeds and/or a dollop of vegan sriracha mayo. Add a side of fruit and make it a meal. Serves 2 - 3

title: Loaded Avocado Toast
ingredients:
4 slices white bread
1 avocado
300g mushrooms
1 ripe plantain
2 spring onions
1 Kallo umami stock cube
Himalayan pink salt
Garlic granules
Onion granules
Smoked paprika
Vegetable oil
Sugar
Margarine
preparation:
Method
Slice and roughly chop the mushrooms and put them in a pan.
Add the umami cube, salt, onion granules, smoked paprika and plenty of garlic granules.
Add about 1 tbsp vegetable oil, mix everything together, warm briefly over a low heat so the stock cube dissolves into the seasoning, then take off the heat and leave to marinate for 10 minutes.
Heat the air fryer.
Slice and roughly chop the plantain, put it in a pan with some caster sugar and vegetable oil, and cook over a medium heat until evenly browned.
Return the mushrooms to a medium heat until the marinade is absorbed more and they are less wet, then transfer them to the air fryer to finish cooking until browned and starting to crisp without drying out.
Mash the avocado in a bowl to a chunky consistency and chop the spring onions.
Toast the bread and spread with margarine.
Spread the avocado evenly over the toast, then add the mushrooms and the plantain.
Sprinkle with the chopped spring onions.
Serves 4 slices.

title: Hot Cross Buns
ingredients:
1tbsp ground flaxseed
3tbsp warm water
200ml coconut milk
400g strong white flour
1/4 tsp salt
2tsp cinnamon
1tsp mixed spice
50g caster sugar
7g sachet fast action dried yeast
25g vegan margarine
125g dried mixed fruit
For the crosses:
25g flour
Water, a few tbsp
For the glaze:
3tbsp apricot jam
preparation:
Method
Make the flax egg by mixing the flaxseed with the water. Set aside.
Heat the coconut milk very gently to warm, then set aside.
Sift the flour, salt, cinnamon and mixed spice into a large bowl. Stir in the caster sugar and the yeast.
Rub in the margarine using your fingertips until the mixture resembles breadcrumbs.
Add the dried fruit and stir through.
Mix the flax egg into the warm coconut milk and pour into the dry ingredients.
Use a knife to bring the ingredients together into a dough, then turn the dough out onto a lightly floured work surface and knead well for 10 mins until smooth and elastic.
Place the dough into a large lightly oiled bowl, cover with clingfilm and leave to rise for at least 2 hours or until doubled in size.
Divide the dough into 12 equal sized pieces and shape into smooth balls. Place the balls onto a lined baking tray leaving about 1cm between each bun. Cover the buns loosely with clingfilm and leave to rise for another 30 mins.
To make the crosses, mix the flour with enough water to form a thick white paste, place the paste into a piping bag and pipe a cross over each bun.
Bake the buns in the oven on 200c fan for 20mins until golden brown.
Heat the apricot jam in a little pan to loosen it, then brush this over the buns while they are still hot. Enjoy!

title: The Best Berry Banana Smoothie
ingredients:
Plant milk of your choice - just under a large glass full (I usually opt for rice or almond)
A handful of frozen berries (mixed bag or blueberries are my favourite)
1 x banana
2 x tablespoons of dairy free yoghurt (Alpro Vanilla is great)
1 x tablespoon of chia seeds
preparation:
Preparation
Special thanks to Victoria Harley for the image: split-pin.co.uk
Frozen berries are my secret smoothie weapon! So much cheaper and longer-lasting than buying fresh (for smoothie making). Plus they make the smoothie lovely and chilled, so it's a win/win all round. You'll find bags of these in the freezer section of most supermarkets.
Pop all the ingredients into your blender and blend until smooth. Easy.

title: Pineapple Coconut Muffins
ingredients:
- 2 cups of spelt flour
- 2 tsp of baking soda
- 4 tbsp of coconut powder
- 1 cup of stevia or agave powder
- 400g fresh, ripe pineapple or 1 can of pineapple (400g)
- 2/3 cup molten soya margarine or groundnut oil
- ? cup Provamel Rice Coconut drink
- 1 pinch of salt
- 1 tsp of vanilla extract
preparation:
Preparation
Serves 8.
Pineapple Coconut Muffins are made using Provamel Rice Coconut drink.
Method
Preheat the oven to 180 degrees C. Put all the dry ingredients in a bowl and mix well. Dice the pineapple and puree with a hand blender until you have a coarse paste. Mix the pineapple and the other wet ingredients to the flour. Fill the muffin cases almost to the top. Bake for 25 minutes. Serve warm or cold.

title: Nutty Bananalicious Overnight Oats
ingredients:
500ml Provamel soya alternative to yoghurt free from sugars (or similar)
1 banana, sliced
100g oat flakes
3tbsp crunchy peanut butter
1tbsp chia seeds
preparation:
Method
Mix the yoghurt, banana, oat flakes and chia seeds and let them rest in the fridge overnight. Serve with crunchy peanut butter, banana and red fruits.

title: Aloo Masala Toast
ingredients:
1 tbsp oil
1/2 tsp cumin seeds
3/4 tsp mustard seeds
1/2 tsp chana dal (optional)
1 large onion, thinly sliced
4 cloves garlic, minced
1 thumb size ginger, finely minced
10 curry leaves (optional)
3 green chillies, chopped
1 large tomato, chopped
1/2 tsp turmeric powder
1 tsp salt
4 medium sized potatoes, boiled
small handful of coriander, chopped
8 slices wholemeal bread
vegan butter
1 cup besan/chickpea flour
1/2 tsp red chilli powder
1/2 tsp salt
large pinch turmeric powder
water as required
preparation:
Preparation
Cook the oil in a saucepan and add the cumin seeds, mustard seeds and chana dal. Once they start to splutter, add the onion and saute for 5 minutes till soft. Add garlic and saute for 40 seconds. Mix in the ginger, curry leaves, chillies and chopped tomato. Stir in the turmeric and salt and cook till tomatoes soften.
Add the boiled potatoes and mash everything together until combined. Sprinkle in the coriander.
For the besan/chickpea batter, add the besan flour, chilli powder, salt and turmeric to a bowl. Add enough water to make a thick but spreadable batter.
Heat a tawa/frying pan. Spread the potato mixture on a slice of bread and top with another slice of bread. Dip both sides in the besan batter.
Place on the frying pan and cook for a few minutes on each side until golden brown. Repeat with the remaining bread and potato mixture.

title: Sausage
ingredients: Spinach & Avocado Muffin
preparation:
1 pack Meatless Farm Meat Free Sausages (or similar)
5 medium floury potatoes, peeled and cut into chunks
2 avocados, peeled, halved, stone removed and flesh scooped out
Juice of 1/2 lemon
100g baby spinach
8 English muffins, split and toasted
Oil for brushing
column6:
Method
Preheat the oven to 190C/170C Fan/Gas 5.
Brush a large baking tray with oil. Cut each sausage into 4 pieces and place on the tray. Roast for 10 minutes, turn then roast for a further 5 minutes, or until browned all over.
Meanwhile, put the potatoes in a saucepan and cover with cold water. Bring to the boil, cover, and cook for 10 minutes, or until tender. Drain well and set aside to steam-dry.
Put the avocado flesh in a bowl, add the lemon juice and mash roughly with a fork. Stir in the baby spinach and set aside.
Put the potatoes in the bowl used for the avocado and squash them roughly with the back of a fork.
To assemble, spread the avocado mixture over the muffin bases. Top each with some of the crushed potato and then pieces of sausage. Put the muffin lids on top and serve.

title: Nutty Slices with Strawberry Chia Jam
ingredients:
Strawberry Chia Jam:
2 cups of diced strawberries
1/4 cup maple syrup
3 -4 tbsp of chia seeds
Slice and Crumble:
2 and 1/2 cups of gluten free oats
1 cup peanut butter
1/4 cup maple syrup
1 small apple blended to apple sauce
1 tsp cinnamon
1 flax seed or chia egg ( ? cup of water mixed with ? cup of ground flax/chia)
1 cup flour
A handful of sliced almonds
preparation:
Method
Preheat oven to 160 degrees.
Simmer the Strawberry Chia Jam ingredients on low heat until it thickens. Set aside to cool down
Then make the slices:
Combine oats, apple sauce, cinnamon and 'egg'.
Mix maple syrup and peanut butter together and put in a microwave for 20 seconds.
Pour into the oats and mix.
Reserve 1 cup of crumble and stir in the flour into the remaining mix.
Press into a lined tin, add strawberry jam on top and the crumble and almonds on top.
Bake for 20-25 mins.

title: Nutrition Bar
ingredients:
600g mixed nuts
1 cup chia seeds
1 cup flax seeds
1 cup hemp seeds
1 cup buckwheat flour
5 dates
4 apples
1/4 teaspoon orange zest
2 tablespoons cinnamon
2 tablespoons mixed spice
2 tablespoons of olive oil
preparation:
Preparation
Serves 20.
Method
Place the nuts in a food processor and process until a powder. Add the chia, flax and hemp seeds and blend. Add the buckwheat flour, dates and apples and process to a sticky consistency. Add the orange zest, cinnamon and mixed spice and blend. Stir in the olive oil. Push the mixture down in a baking tin lined with parchment paper and put in the fridge overnight. Cut into bars and serve.

title: Healthy Overnight Oats (Vegan +No-Cook)
ingredients:
1/2 cup rolled oats, quick oats, steel-cut oats (see notes)
1 tablespoon flaxseed meal or chia seeds, optional
1/2 cup plant milk or water
1/4 cup yogurt, optional (see notes)
1/4 cup fruit, fresh or frozen
1 - 2 teaspoons maple syrup, optional
preparation:
Instructions In a small container, mix the oats, optional flaxseed meal or chia seeds, and plant milk together.
Layer top with yogurt and fruit.
Cover and place in the refrigerator overnight.
When ready to eat, stir well, add a few splashes of milk if desired, and top with optional drizzle of maple syrup or peanut butter or toppings of choice.
Serves 1
Store: Keep covered in the refrigerator for 4 - 5 days.
NOTES:
Can substitute yogurt with an extra 2 tablespoons plant milk.
Steel-cut oats will have a chewier texture.
RECOMMENDED EQUIPMENT: Weck Jars, Mason Jars, or reusable prep containers are great options for storing and making overnight oats. For more of my favorite cooking tools, shop my kitchen essentials!

title: Chocolate Almond Butter Smoothie Bowl
ingredients:
2 large frozen bananas (pref. overripe)
2 tablespoons almond butter (or other nut/seed butter)
2 tablespoons cacao or cocoa powder
1/2 teaspoon vanilla, optional
1/2 cup unsweetened almond milk or water, plus more as needed
4 - 5 ice cubes
preparation:
Instructions Place bananas, almond butter, cocoa powder, optional vanilla, milk and ice cubes into a high-speed blender and blend until thick and creamy, adding more liquid or ice cubes as needed.
Pour the smoothie into a bowl or cup, and top with any optional toppings:
granola
fresh berries (or frozen berries)
coconut flakes
sliced almonds
cacao nibs
hemp hearts or chia seeds
Serves 1 large or 2 small
Store: Best used fresh.

title: BEST VEGAN WAFFLES (EASY + HEALTHY)
ingredients:
2 cups (250g) flour (spelt, light whole wheat, all-purpose flour, or GF flour blend)
2 tablespoons sugar
4 teaspoons baking powder
1/4 teaspoon salt
1 1/2 cup (350ml) unsweetened plant milk
1/3 cup (80ml) olive oil, melted coconut oil, or applesauce
2 teaspoons vanilla extract
preparation:
Instructions Prep: Preheat waffle maker according to manufacturer's instructions. Also, preheat oven to 200 degrees F to keep the waffles warm between batches.
Mix: Place the flour, sugar, baking powder, and salt in a mixing bowl, whisk together. Add the milk, oil, and vanilla, mix until combined. Batter will be thin.
Cook Waffles: Add about 1/2 cup batter to the centre of your waffle maker and using the back of the measuring cup, spread the batter around. Cook waffles to your manufacturer's recommended times. Mine took 3 1/2 minutes.
Serve warm with pure maple syrup, fresh fruit, and Cashew Sweet Cream. Makes 4 regular size round waffles. See notes for how to make a half batch.
To Keep Waffles Warm: Place waffles directly on rack in preheated oven until ready to serve.
To Store: Let waffles cool completely and keep in a covered container in the refrigerator for up to 5 days. For longer storage, keep in the freezer for up to 2 - 3 months.

title: BEST PUMPKIN OATMEAL (HEALTHY + EASY)
ingredients:
1 cup old fashioned oats
1/2 cup pumpkin puree
1 teaspoon pumpkin pie spice (or cinnamon)
1/4 cup plant milk (I used oat milk)
1 1/2 cups water
pinch of salt
To serve
1 - 2 tablespoons pure maple syrup
1 - 2 tablespoons non-dairy milk (I used oat milk), optional
1/4 - 1/2 cup pecans, optional
preparation:
Instructions Stovetop: In a saucepan, combine oats, pumpkin puree, pumpkin spice, plant milk , water, and salt, bring to a boil, reduce heat to simmer and cook over a gentle boil for 3 - 4 minutes. Remove from heat and let stand for 2 to 3 minutes.
Microwave: Add ingredients in a large bowl and mix well to combine. Microwave on HIGH for 3 minutes, stopping to stir after 2 minutes. To prevent the oatmeal from boiling over, be sure to use a larger, wider bowl. Let cool a few minutes before serving.
Instant Pot: Combine oats, pumpkin puree, pumpkin spice, plant milk, water, and salt into the bottom of an Instant Pot insert. Give a good stir to mix in the pumpkin puree.
Pressure cook: Secure lid and set valve to sealing position. Select Manual/Pressure Cook button on HIGH for 3 minutes. When done, let natural release for 7 minutes, and manually release pressure. Remove lid and let oatmeal cool a bit before serving.
Serve: Spoon oatmeal into individual bowls, and top with your favorite toppings.
Makes about 2 servings
Store: Leftovers can be stored in the refrigerator for up to 5 - 6 days, in a sealed container. Reheat on stovetop or in microwave.

title: Buckwheat Banana Bread
ingredients:
1 3/4 cups (210 g) buckwheat flour
1/3 cup (75 g) sugar (pure cane, coconut sugar or maple syrup)
2 heaping teaspoons cinnamon
2 heaping teaspoons baking powder
pinch of mineral salt
4 large, very ripe bananas, mashed (about 1 3/4 (410 g))
1/3 cup (75 ml) light flavored olive oil, coconut oil (in liquid form) or hazelnut oil
2 teaspoons vanilla extract
preparation:
Instructions Preheat oven to 350 degrees F.
In a medium/large mixing bowl, combine the buckwheat flour, sugar, cinnamon, baking powder and salt, mix well. Add the mashed bananas, oil and vanilla, mix to combine. Place batter in a lightly greased 9?5 loaf pan and bake on the middle rack for 50 minutes. Remove from oven, let cool about 10 minutes and enjoy.
Pairs wonderfully with a little homemade almond butter!
Store: Leftovers can be stored covered on the counter for up to 3 days or in the refrigerator for up to 1 week. Keep in the freezer for up to 3 months - place completely cooled loaves in freezer containers or bags. Thaw the wrapped loaves overnight in the refrigerator.
Makes 8 - 10 slices

title: HEALTHY HOMEMADE GRANOLA RECIPE (OIL-FREE)
ingredients:
3 cups old fashioned oats
3/4 cup almonds, rough chopped
3/4 cup walnuts, rough chopped
1/2 cup pepitas or sunflower seeds
1/2 cup dried cranberries or raisins
1/2 cup unsweetened applesauce
1/3 cup pure maple syrup
1 teaspoon vanilla extract
2 teaspoons cinnamon
1/4 teaspoon sea salt
preparation:
Instructions Prep: Preheat oven to 300 degrees Fahrenheit. Line large, rimmed baking sheet with parchment paper or a silpat.
Mix dry ingredients: In large bowl, mix oats, nuts, seeds, cranberries, cinnamon and salt.
Mix wet ingredients: In small bowl, combine applesauce, maple syrup and vanilla.
Combine: Add wet ingredients to dry ingredients, mix until thoroughly combined.
Bake: Spread granola evenly over baking sheet. Bake for 35 - 40 minutes, stirring every 10 minutes. Granola is done when lightly golden. Let cool before serving or storing.
Makes about 5 cups
Store: Keep granola in airtight containers on the counter for up to 2 weeks or the refrigerator for up to 1 month.
Serving suggestion: Enjoy this easy granola with your favorite plant-based milk or non-dairy yogurt and sliced fruit!

title: Healthy Oatmeal Recipe (Vegan)
ingredients:
2 cups oats (old fashioned or quick)
3 1/2 cups water (or milk)
pinch mineral salt
preparation:
Instructions Instant Pot: Combine 2 cups of oats with 3 1/2 cups of water (or double the amounts) in the bottom of the insert. Secure lid with valve in sealing position. Cook on manual high pressure for 3 minutes. Let pressure release naturally for 7 minutes. Serve right away or cool, place in storage containers and keep in the refrigerator. Alternately, you can make overnight oatmeal by using the keep warm setting (see notes).
Stove Top: Add oats, water/milk and salt to a saucepan, bring to a boil, reduce heat to low and cook at a low boil, stirring often, for 4 - 5 minutes.
Microwave: Place oats, water and salt in a large microwave safe bowl, microwave on high for 2 - 3 minutes, stopping to stir after 1 1/2 minutes. To prevent overflow, use a large bowl.
Makes 4 servings
Store: Keep leftovers in the refrigerator for up to 5 days.

title: BLUEBERRY BANANA SMOOTHIE
ingredients:
1 frozen banana
1 cup frozen blueberries
1 tablespoon nut butter (peanut, cashew, or almond butter)
1/4 - 1/2 teaspoon cinnamon
1 cup unsweetened almond milk (plain or vanilla flavored)
preparation:
Instructions Place all ingredients in blender and blend to desired consistency adding more milk or water as needed.
Serves 1

title: Mango Pineapple Smoothie
ingredients:
1 mango (about 1 - 1 1/2 cups), cut into chunks (fresh or frozen)
1 ripe banana
1 cup pineapple chunks (fresh or frozen)
1/2 cup almond milk or water
preparation:
Instructions Combine mango, banana, pineapple, and water/milk into a blender cup and blend until smooth, adding more liquid as needed.
Serves 1

title: VEGAN PUMPKIN OAT MUFFINS + CRUMBLE TOPPING
ingredients:
1 1/2 cups light spelt flour
1/2 cup packed organic brown sugar
2 teaspoons baking powder
1 1/2 teaspoons pumpkin pie spice
1/2 teaspoon baking soda
pinch of salt
1 1/4 cups 100% pure pumpkin puree
1/2 cup almond milk (room temp)
1/3 cup coconut oil (in liquid state) or applesauce
1 1/2 cups old-fashioned oats
1/4 cup barberries or currents, optional
Topping
1/3 cup packed organic brown sugar
1 tablespoon flour
3/4 teaspoon cinnamon (or pumpkin spice)
1 tablespoon solid coconut oil or vegan butter/margarine
preparation:
Instructions Preheat oven to 375 degrees F.
In a large bowl, add the flour, sugar, baking powder, pumpkin spice, baking soda, and salt, and mix to combine. Add the milk, oil, and pumpkin to the dry ingredients and mix just until moistened. Stir in oats and raisins. Fill each muffin space, just below the rim, with the pumpkin batter.
In a small bowl, combine the brown sugar, flour, and pumpkin pie spice, add oil/butter and cut into sugar mixture until crumbly. Sprinkle 1 heaping teaspoonful over top each muffin.
Bake in oven on middle rack for 20 - 25 minutes, or until a toothpick comes out clean.
Let cool 5 minutes before removing from pan to a wire rack. Serve warm.
Store covered loosely in a container on the counter for 4 days, or keep longer by storing in the refrigerator.

title: Avocado on Toast
ingredients:
1 large slice of bread
1 large ripe avocado
Juice of 1 lemon
Extra virgin olive oil
Sprinkle of Japanese Nanami Togarashi or chilli flakes
Basil and tomatoes (to serve)
preparation:
Method
This is really quick, first job is to put your toast on.
While that is going on squash the flesh of the avocado in a small bowl using a fork.  You don't want this to be too smooth, a bit of texture is more satisfying.
Mix the lemon juice into the avocado to stop it going brown.
As soon as the toast is ready give it a quick drizzle of olive oil and then spread over the avocado.
Lastly, sprinkle with the Nanami Togarash or chilli flakes, and serve with fresh tomatoes and basil

title: Apple and Pear Rosti
ingredients:
2 baking potatoes
1 large apple
1 pear
Rock salt
Cooking oil
A pinch of nutmeg, chilli flakes or your own preferred seasoning
preparation:
Method
Peel the potatoes and chop into halves and boil for 7 minutes. Drain the potatoes and give them time to cool enough for you to be able to hold them without burning yourself! Then grate the potatoes. Peel and grate the apple and pear and them mix them with the grated potatoes. You need to get as much moisture out of the mix as you can- squeeze it, pat it with a towel. Add salt and your seasonings. Separate the mix into 4 equal parts and make circles with them; think burger shaped. Really try and press the mix together to help it bind. Add enough oil to a frying pan to cover the bottom and fry the rostis for roughly 8-10 minutes each side. But use your judgement here, you want them starting to crisp on the outside.
NOTE: Now in theory the rostis should be done now. However if they are not binding well (and they don't always) then put them in a preheated oven for 5 minutes to finish them off.

title: Makai Pawa
ingredients:
3 handfuls of Pawa (rice flakes)
Less than 1/2 a tin of sweetcorn (Makai) - about 120g
1 fresh chilli
Less than 1 tablespoon of oil - sunflower, olive, canola or rapeseed oil
1/2 teaspoon of mustard seeds
2 sweet neem Leaves
1 pinch of asafetida (hing)
Less than a 1/4 teaspoon of cumin seeds (jiru)
1 handful of red skin peanut kernels
1 pinch of turmeric
Salt to taste
1 drizzle of lemon juice
1 handful of freshly chopped coriander
preparation:
Preparation
This recipe serves 1
Method
Wash 3 handfuls of Pawa (rice flakes) in warm water - do this once or twice but not for long
Leave it in a strainer until you need it, so that the water drains away
In a pan, add the oil, mustard seeds, jiru and hing
Add the peanut kernels
Add 2 sweet neem leaves
Put the lid on the pan and wait until the seeds begin to pop
Add the chilli - ensure that you taste the chilli first so that you can amend how much you put in according to how spicy the chilli is
Open a can of sweetcorn and drain the water
Add the sweetcorn to the pan and mix
Add salt to taste
Add the turmeric
Let it cook until the corn is softer (about 5 minutes)
Add the Pawa and lemon juice and mix
After about 5 minutes add the chopped coriander and mix
Serve and enjoy after a couple of minutes

title: Yoghurt and Fruit Snack
ingredients:
Your favourite, single serving vegan yoghurt (I picked Mango COYO)
Your favourite fruit (apple, banana, grapes, orange... the list is endless!)
preparation:
Preparation
Special thanks to Victoria Harley for the image: split-pin.co.uk
Method
No instructions necessary ??

title: Tofu Bhurji (Indian Scrambled Tofu)
ingredients:
? tsp cumin seeds
? tsp fennel seeds
1 tbsp oil
400 g tofu
2 medium onions chopped
1 bell pepper chopped
2 green chillies chopped
? tbsp grated garlic
1 tsp grated ginger
1 large tomato chopped
1 tsp pink salt
1 tsp ground cumin
1 tsp ground coriander
? tsp ground turmeric
2 tsp Kashmiri chilli powder
1/2 juice from a lemon
1/8 tsp ground cinnamon
? cup chopped coriander
preparation:
Method
Squeeze excess water out of the tofu. Crumble the tofu into small-medium sized pieces. Set aside. Heat oil in a pan over medium heat. Add in cumin seeds, fennel seeds and mix. Mix in chopped onions and green chillies and saute onions until translucent. Add grated garlic and grated ginger and cook for 2 minutes or until the raw smell disappears. Add chopped bell pepper and chopped tomato and cook for another 1-2 minutes. Add in ground cumin, ground coriander, ground turmeric, pink salt, Kashmiri chilli powder and cook for 2-3 minutes. Add in crumbled tofu, mix well and cook for another 2 minutes. Mix in ground cinnamon. Squeeze juice lemon and add chopped coriander. Take off heat and serve with chapati / naan.

title: Traditional Irish Porridge Bread
ingredients:
400g of gluten-free oats (equivalent to two yoghurt cartons filled with oats. We used Bunalun)
1 500g tub of Alpro plain yoghurt (or equivalent)
1 tsp baking soda
? tsp salt
1 tbsp soya milk (or any plant-based milk you want)
1 tbsp treacle
A handful of mixed seeds
preparation:
Preparation
Preheat oven to 180 c/ 350 f/ Gas mark 4.
Method
Line your bread tin with greaseproof paper or grease it with oil or soya butter. Add the full tub of Alpro yoghurt to a large bowl. Fill the empty yoghurt tub with oats and pour that amount into the bowl with the yoghurt. Scrape out any bits sticking to the sides. Repeat this step so you should have 2 yoghurt tub measures full of porridge oats in the bowl. Add all the rest of your ingredients to the bowl too. Fold all your ingredients together. If it seems a bit dry add another tbsp of milk until the mixture is like a wet oaty dough. When the mixture is well combined add it to your bread tin and smooth the top. Sprinkle the seeds over the top. Bake for approx. 45 - 55 minutes and when it is 10 minutes from the end take the bread out of the tin and turn it upside down in the tine and put back into the oven. Cook it like that for the last 10 minutes. When it's ready take it out and turn it upside down on a wire rack and leave it until it's cool. Then enjoy!

title: Zingy Avocado on Toast
ingredients:
4 slices multi-grain bread
2 avocados, ready to eat
5 tbsp Alpro Plain soya alternative to Greek style yogurt
1 tsp chilli flakes
3 tsp lemon juice
Pepper and a pinch of salt
preparation:
Preparation
Toast the bread
Peel, stone and mash the avocado flesh with the lemon juice
Add the Alpro Plain soya alternative to Greek Style yogurt and chilli flakes, season to taste and stir until everything's well mixed
Spread the avocado chilli mix on the toast, sprinkled with some extra chilli flakes, if you like it hot!

title: Sweet Porridge with Nuts, Seeds and Dried Fruit
ingredients:
3 tablespoons of porridge oats
1/2 mug of water
1/4/ mug of milk - rice milk, oat milk, soya milk or hemp milk
1 pinch of cardamom
1 tablespoon of seeds and 1 tablespoon of fruit - I used the following
Pumpkin seeds
Dried mango
Dried papaya
Raisins
Brazil nuts
preparation:
Method
Put half a mug of water in a pan - you can also add 1/4 of a mug of milk OR replace the water with milk
Add the cardamom
Add the oats
Let it boil until it reaches the consistency you like
Serve in a bowl
Add some pumpkin seeds, dried cranberries, pecan nuts, chopped walnuts and any other nuts or dried/fresh fruit that you desire
Enjoy!

title: Tempeh Bacon Avocado Toast
ingredients:
For the Tempeh Bacon:
1 block Plant Power Original Tempeh
2 tbsp tamari
1 tbsp maple syrup
? -1 tsp liquid smoke (depends on taste, use more if you like a very smoky flavour)
1 tbsp oil for frying
To serve
Avocado
Bread
Toasted seeds (optional)
preparation:
Preparation
Slice tempeh into thin 0.5 - 1cm slices.
Heat a frying pan and add the oil. Once the oil is hot, add the tamari, maple syrup and liquid smoke. Fry the tempeh strips on each side for about 2 to 3 minutes, ensuring both sides are lightly browned, turning every so often until the edges look caramelized.
Whilst the tempeh is frying, mash an avocado until smooth and toast the bread.
Add a thick layer of smashed avocado to the toast and then add the tempeh bacon on top. Sprinkle over the toasted seeds.
Recipe provided by: www.plant-power.co.uk

title: Super Green Detox Smoothie by Christine Bailey
ingredients:
Large handful of spinach or chopped kale leaves
1 celery stalk chopped
? banana, chopped (preferably frozen for creamier texture)
Juice of ? lemon
Few stalks of parsley leaves
1 scoop Pulsin vegan protein powder, optional
? chopped cucumber
1 small ripe pear cored and chopped
250ml cooled green tea or coconut water
1 tsp chia seeds
1 tsp supergreen powder
preparation:
Method
Place all the ingredients in a blender and process until smooth and creamy. Best drunk straight away.

title: Low Sugar Raspberry Chia Jam
ingredients:
150g raspberries, fresh or frozen
2-3 tbsp coconut water or water
1tbsp xylitol, optional
2 heaped tbsp chia seeds
preparation:
Method
Place all of the ingredients in a small pan and simmer gently for 2-3 minutes to thicken. Mash down the fruit with a wooden spoon as it cooks. You may need to add a little more water if the mixture is too dry.
For a smoother texture you can then process briefly in a food processor or blender.
Allow to cool then store in the fridge until required. It will keep in the fridge for 3-4 days or you can freeze it for up to 3 months.

title: Green Juice
ingredients:
1 whole pineapple including the core
1 unwaxed lemon including the peel cut into small pieces
Broccoli, including the stems and leaves (quantity varies depending on how green you'd like it)
preparation:
Preparation
Juicer
Method
Put everything through the high-powered juicer.
Serve with a slice of lemon.

title: VEGGIE TOFU SCRAMBLE WITH DIJON
ingredients:
1 tablespoon olive oil
1/2 onion, diced
1 block (14-16 oz.) tofu, firm or extra firm (drained, no need to press)
8 oz. package mushrooms, sliced or quartered (about 1 1/2 - 2 cups)
1 1/2 cups broccoli florets
2 tablespoons dijon mustard
1 teaspoon dried tarragon, optional
1 teaspoon garlic powder
himalayan salt & pepper, to taste
2 handfuls fresh spinach
1 - 2 tablespoons nutritional yeast, optional
preparation:
Instructions In skillet or wok, heat oil over medium heat, add onions and saute for 4 minutes. Add the tofu, crumbling it between your fingers, and cook for another 3 minutes.
Add mushrooms, broccoli, dijon, tarragon, garlic powder, salt and pepper, cook another 5 minutes.
Add the spinach and continue to cook over medium heat, stirring occasionally until spinach starts to wilt, about 3 - 5 minutes. Remove from heat. Season to taste, adding the optional nutritional yeast.
Serve as you like adding whatever extra spices or condiments you wish. I served mine with toast with avocado butter and fresh fruit on the side for a refreshing touch.
Serves 3 - 4
Store: Leftovers can be kept in the refrigerator for up to 4 days, in a covered container.

title: SEEDS & GOJI BERRY GRANOLA
ingredients:
2 1/2 cups old fashioned rolled oats (I used GF)
1/3 cup raw sunflower seeds
1/3 cup pepitas (pumpkin seeds)
1/3 cup goji berries
2 tablespoons chia seeds
2 tablespoons hemp hearts
1 teaspoon cinnamon
pinch of salt
2/3 cup pure maple syrup*
1/4 cup sunbutter or tahini, optional
1 teaspoon vanilla
preparation:
Instructions Preheat oven to 250 degrees. Line a rimmed baking sheet with parchment or silicone mat, or lightly grease with coconut oil.
Combine all dry ingredients in a medium size bowl. Add maple syrup, sunbutter/tahini and vanilla. Mix until well coated.
Spread granola, in a single layer, on baking sheet.
Place in oven and bake for 30- 35 minutes, stirring the mixture every 10 minutes to ensure even cooking. If using two baking sheets, you may even like to switch their positions in the oven once after stirring. If using a smaller baking dish such as a 9 ? 9 or 11 ? 13, bake for up to 1 hour or so, or until golden throughout. Be sure to stir the mixture every 15 minutes or so.
Once done, remove from oven and let cool, granola will harden as it cools.
Makes 4 cups.
Store in a covered container on the counter for up to 1 month.

title: GINGER-ORANGE GRANOLA
ingredients:
2 1/2 cups rolled oats
2/3 cup raw almonds
1/2 cup raw sunflower seeds
1/3 cup coconut (flakes or shredded)
3 tablespoons sesame seeds
3 tablespoons hemp hearts, ground flax seeds or chia seeds
1 heaping teaspoon ground ginger
1/2 teaspoon cinnamon
pinch of salt
zest of 1 - 2 oranges or 4 - 5 cuties, chopped fine or left as is
2/3 - 3/4 cups pure maple syrup
preparation:
Instructions Preheat oven to 275 degrees F. Line a rimmed baking sheet with parchment or silicone mat, or lightly grease with coconut oil.
Combine: Mix all of your dry ingredients together in a large mixing bowl. Pour the maple syrup overtop and mix well to coat.
Layer: Spread the granola in a single layer on baking sheet.
Bake: Place sheets in oven and cook for 30 - 40 minutes, stirring every 10 minutes or so to ensure even toasting.
Let cool: Once done, remove from oven, let cool completely, granola will stiffen as it cools.
Makes 4 cups.
Store: Keep leftovers in an airtight container for up to a month.

title: BREAKFAST POTATOES
ingredients:
2 lbs. potatoes (use your favorite), scrubbed and dried, cut into 1/2 -3/4 inch cubes.
1 tablespoon olive oil
1 teaspoon garlic powder
1/2 teaspoon paprika (regular or smoked)
1/2 teaspoon salt, or to taste
fresh cracked pepper, to taste
preparation:
Instructions Preheat oven to 425 degrees F.
Place cubed potatoes on the center of a rimmed baking sheet lined with parchment paper or silicone mat (no liner is fine too). Drizzle with olive oil, garlic powder, paprika, salt and pepper. Using your fingers and hands, toss the potatoes well and spread into a single layer. (Alternatively, you can add everything to a bowl, toss, and place on the baking sheet.)
Place in the oven, on the center rack, and bake for 25 minutes, stirring once.
Turn oven to 500 degrees F. and roast another 10 - 15 minutes, stirring once until edges are golden and crispy.
Let cool a few minutes and serve right away.
Serves 4 - 6
Store: Leftovers can be stored in the refrigerator for up to 4 - 5 days, in a covered container. To keep longer, freeze the potatoes for up to 2 months in freezer safe containers or ziplock bags.

title: FAVORITE CRANBERRY ORANGE BREAD
ingredients:
2 cups (240 g) flour (light spelt, white whole wheat or all-purpose)
2/3 cup (133 g) organic pure cane sugar
2 teaspoons baking powder
1/2 teaspoon baking soda
generous pinch of salt
1 cup orange juice
1/2 cup (113 g) unsweetened applesauce
1 heaping cup (129 g) fresh or frozen cranberries, plus more for the top*
Tasty optional add-ins:
1 - 2 teaspoons vanilla (add with orange juice)
3/4 teaspoon cinnamon or ginger powder (add with dry ingredients)
1 - 2 tablespoons orange zest, optional (add with cranberries)
preparation:
Instructions
Preheat oven to 350 degrees F. Lightly grease a 9 x 5 loaf pan.
In a mixing bowl, add the flour, sugar, baking powder, baking soda and salt, stir to combine.
Add in the applesauce, orange juice and optional vanilla, mix until flour is incorporated, don't over mix, stir in the cranberries and optional orange zest, give a few extra stirs.
Pour batter into prepared loaf pan, strategically arrange a few more cranberries on top, and place on center rack in the oven, bake for  50 - 55 minutes.
Remove from oven, let cool and enjoy.
Store, covered, on the counter for up to 3 days, or keep in the refrigerator for up to 1 week. Also, you can freeze your bread, stored in a freezer safe container for up to 2 months, let thaw in the refrigerator.

title: CARROT, PINEAPPLE & GINGER JUICE
ingredients:
7 - 9 large carrots
1/4 pineapple (about 1 cup), flesh only
1-inch knob of ginger, peeled
preparation:
Instructions Prep: Scrub and rinse the carrots well. If the tops are clean-looking, you can leave them on. If not, trim off the very top 1/2 inch or so. If using carrots with the leafy green tops, you can juice the whole thing, but your juice color may change.
Juice according to your equipment and enjoy.
Store: The juice will keep nicely in an airtight container for up to 48 hours in the fridge but is best fresh within a few hours. Give a good stir before drinking.
Makes about 2 cups.
Severs 2

title: Chia Seed Smoothie
ingredients:
1 cup frozen fruit (strawberries, blueberries, mango, cherry, pineapple, etc.)
1 fresh ripe banana (see notes)
1/2 - 3/4 cup coconut water or non-dairy milk (almond, oat, coconut, cashew, etc.)
2 tablespoons chia seeds
preparation:
Instructions Place the frozen fruit, banana, liquids, and chia seeds in the blender cup, attach lid, and blend to desired smoothness. Add more liquids as needed.
Makes one generous or two small servings.

title: INSTANT POT STEEL CUT OATS
ingredients:
1 cups steel cut oats
2 1/2 cups water
pinch of salt
Optional add-ins:
1 teaspoon vanilla
1/2 teaspoon cinnamon
1/4 teaspoon cardamom
To Serve
2 tablespoon pure maple syrup, per serving
1/4 cup unsweetened almond milk (or oat, cashew, soy, or your favorite plant milk)
handful of berries (strawberries, blueberries, raspberries, or blackberries)
1 - 2 tablespoons nuts or seeds (almonds, walnuts, pecans, pumpkin seeds, hemp hearts, etc.)
preparation:
Instructions Add the steel cut oats, optional spices & vanilla, pinch of salt, and water to the Instant Pot insert. Attach lid and twist to lock. Turn the valve to SEALING position. Manually set to HIGH pressure and cook for 4 minutes. Let natural release for 15 minutes.
Serve in individual serving bowls or glass containers, and top with your choice of toppings, pure maple syrup and non-dairy milk.
Makes about 2 cups (2/3 - 1 cup per serving)
Serves 2 - 3
Store: Leftovers can be kept in the refrigerator for up to 4 - 5 days. Warm on the stovetop over low, adding a splash of water or milk, until warmed through.

title: Acai Smoothie
ingredients:
1 tablespoon acai powder or 1 packet acai berry puree
1 cup frozen mixed berries
1 large frozen banana, sliced
3/4 cup unsweetened almond milk, plain or vanilla
1 tablespoon hemp seeds or flax meal, optional
preparation:
Instructions Place the frozen banana, berries, acai powder or puree, optional seeds, and liquids into the blender cup.
Puree until smooth and creamy, about 1 minute, adding a little extra liquid as needed to help it move along and blend.
Pour into a serving glass and enjoy!
Serves 1

title: APPLE CINNAMON MUFFINS (GLUTEN FREE)
ingredients:
2 1/4 cups almond flour*
1/2 cup quick oats
pinch of mineral salt
1 teaspoon baking powder
1 teaspoon cinnamon
2 flax eggs or 1/2 cup unsweetened applesauce
1/4 cup pure maple syrup
1/4 cup light flavored olive oil
1/4 cup unsweetened almond milk, or non-dairy milk of choice
1 teaspoon vanilla extract
1 medium apple (about 3/4 cup), finely diced or grated
1/2 cup chopped pecans or walnuts, optional
garnish
2 tablespoons coconut sugar, pure cane sugar or turbinado
1/2 teaspoon cinnamon
tablespoon quick oats, optional
preparation:
Instructions Preheat oven to 375 degrees. Line a muffin tin using 10 liners.
Flax egg: In a small bowl combine 2 tablespoons flaxseed meal with 6 tablespoons water. Let set for 10 min to thicken.
Topping: In a small bowl, combine the sugar and cinnamon, set aside.
Combine dry ingredients: In a large bowl, combine the almond flour, oats, salt, baking powder and cinnamon, give a stir to mix.
Add in the wet ingredients: To the dry ingredients, add the flax egg, maple syrup, oil, plant milk and vanilla, mix until just combined. Stir in the apples, mix again. Batter will not be runny and should be on the thicker side.
Fill muffin tin: Scoop and fill each muffin cup just to the top. Sprinkle with cinnamon sugar topping. Bake for 20 minutes, or until toothpick comes out clean and muffins are lightly browned on top. Let cool a bit, remove muffins to finish cooling on a cooling rack.
Makes 10 muffins
Storing: Freshly baked muffins will keep well loosely covered, on the counter for 4 - 5 days. Or once completely cooled, keep them in the refrigerator for up to 8 - 10 days. If storing in a container, let muffins cool completely before storing.

title: VEGAN CRANBERRY ORANGE MUFFINS
ingredients:
2 cups flour
3/4 cup sugar (pure cane, coconut or date)
2 teaspoons baking powder
1/2 teaspoon baking soda
pinch of salt
1 cup orange juice
1/2 cup unsweetened applesauce
2 cups cranberries (fresh or frozen)
1 tablespoon orange zest
preparation:
Instructions Preheat the oven to 350 degrees F. Line a muffin tin with parchment liners or lightly grease with oil.
Mix: In a medium mixing bowl, whisk together the flour, sugar, baking powder, baking soda and salt. Add the orange juice and applesauce, mix well until just combined, don't overmix. Fold in the cranberries and orange zest.
Fill Muffin Tin: Spoon the batter into the muffin tin holes, filling to the rim. Optionally, top with a few cranberries as desired and a sprinkle of sugar for a crunchy top.
Bake: Place on the center rack and bake for 30 minutes. Once done, let cool a few minutes and enjoy!
Store: Muffins will keep on the counter, loosely covered, for up to 3 days. For longer storage keep in the refrigerator for up to 6 days, or freeze for up to 2 - 3 months.

title: Lemon Sesame Seed Protein Balls
ingredients:
185g (one heaping cup) of dried, pitted dates
2 tablespoons of sesame seeds
25g (1/4 cup) of pistachios
Juice of 1/3 lemon
2 tablespoons of agave syrup
30g (1/3 cup) of gluten-free rolled oats
2 teaspoons of Pulsin' Organic Sunflower Protein Powder (or your favorite protein powder)
preparation:
Preparation
After struggling with undiagnosed chronic pain for months, Michaella Mazzoni turned to food for healing. Michaella's kitchen is a food blog with recipes which are all refined sugar free. All the recipes are wholesome, healthy and easily replicated in reader's homes without expensive equipment or hard to find ingredients. Michaella Mazzoni
Method
Preheat your oven to 180 degrees Celsius. Line a baking tray with parchment paper and evenly spread out the oats. Place in the oven on the middle tray for about 5-7 minutes or until the oats become golden brown in colour. While the oats are happily toasting away, start to chop the dates until they become a paste like consistency. Roughly chop the pistachios. Next, mix together the chopped dates with the agave syrup, seeds, pistachios and protein powder. Lastly, stir in the toasted oats and the fresh lemon juice. Roll the mixture together into balls and store in the fridge in an air tight container.
Like this recipe? Sign up to Veganuary today to get recipes like this delivered directly to your inbox.

title: Baked Carrot Bacon
ingredients:
6-8 large carrots
1/2 cup vegetable broth
1 teaspoon liquid aminos or soy sauce
2 tablespoons olive oil
1/2 teaspoons liquid smoke
1/2 teaspoon smoked paprika
Salt and pepper to taste
preparation:
Method
Preheat your oven to 400F.
Slice carrots into thin strips using either a mandolin or food processor to keep them uniform.
In a small bowl, whisk together broth, liquid aminos or the soy sauce, olive oil, liquid smoke, and smoked paprika. Then, in a shallow dish, toss sliced carrots with broth combination until carrots are completely coated.
Arrange carrot slices on a foil-wrapped baking sheet so that they don't overlap. Bake in the oven for 7 minutes. Pull baking sheet from the oven and flip carrots. Put baking sheet back in the oven to bake for another 7-8 minutes. It might take more or less time depending on how thick or thin you sliced your carrots.
You'll know your carrot bacon is done when the edges are crispy and the center of each slice looks dry.
Move carrot bacon to a plate with a paper towel to soak up any extra oil. Salt and pepper to taste.
Let cool to room temperature before crumbling over a salad or putting in a sandwich. Store in an airtight container in the fridge.

title: Berry and Banana Muffins
ingredients:
275g gluten/wheat free plain flour
1 tablespoon gluten free baking powder
? teaspoon salt
1 teaspoon bicarbonate of soda
1 teaspoon xanthan gum
2 tablespoons mixed seeds, such as pumpkin, sunflower and/or linseed
130g bag N?kd Berry Delight Fruit & Nut Bits, roughly chopped
2 bananas, roughly chopped
300ml soya milk
100g dairy free soya spread, melted
2 tablespoons fruit syrup
150g blueberries
preparation:
Preparation
Makes: 12
Preparation time: 10 minutes
Cooking time: 15 minutes
Method
Preheat the oven to 190 degrees C, Gas Mark 6
Next, line a twelve-hole muffin tray with paper cases
In a large bowl, stir together the flour, baking powder, bicarbonate of soda, salt, xanthan gum, seeds and N?kd Berry Delight Fruit & Nut Bits
In a separate bowl, mash the bananas with a fork and add the milk, melted spread and fruit syrup. Mix well.
Stir the wet ingredients into the dry and mix until just combined, then fold in the blueberries
Divide the mixture between the paper cases and bake for 15 minutes or until the tops are lightly browned and spring back when pressed gently.
Leave to stand until cool

title: Vegan French Toast
ingredients:
1 Jar Clearspring Amazake - Brown Rice
1 Jar filtered water
1 tbsp Clearspring Coconut oil
1 baguette or a loaf of sough dough bread (optional Gluten Free bread)
1 tsp Clearspring Kuzu (optional)
2 tbsp Maple Syrup (optional)
Fresh Fruit (optional)
Clearspring Fruit Puree if fresh fruit is not in season (optional)
preparation:
Preparation
Take your bread and cut to around 3cm thick with a bread knife or to your prefered thickness.
Empty the Amazake into a large bowl, fill the jar with filtered water and mix both liquids with a whisk or a spoon until blended. (Optional - Mix the Kuzu with 1 tbsp of water and add to the mixture. This will thicken the liquid) Dip the bread into the liquid and place in a large container (with a lid), pour the remaining liquid into the container and leave in the fridge for at least 30min.
Wash and slice all the fruit you would like to have as your toppin and leave in a bowl.
In a large frying pan, place 1 tbsp of coconut oil, one by one, slowly place the amazake soaked bread and fry them on medium to low heat. Flip the bread and check that both sides are golden brown.
Place all the cooked french toast on a serving dish. Now, let's start decorating the dish. Pour a generous amount of maple syrup before serving. Enjoy warm.

title: Psychedelic Smoothie Bowl
ingredients:
1 bananas
1/2c coconut yoghurt
1/2c dairy free milk
? a lemon, zested
1/4t turmeric powder
A handful of fresh or frozen raspberries
1t beetroot powder
A handful of baby spinach
1/2t spirulina powder
preparation:
Method
Blend the bananas, yoghurt and milk till smooth, portion it into 3 bowls. In the first bowl add the lemon zest and turmeric, blend. In the second bowl add the raspberries and beetroot powder, blend. In the third bowl add the spinach and spirulina, blend. In your serving bowl add a large spoonful of each mixture, one at a time. Once all of the smoothie is in your bowl use your finger to create beautiful patterns.

title: Spiced Plum Muffins
ingredients:
150g spelt flour
150g ground almonds
2 tsps ground ginger
1 tsp baking powder
1 tsp bicarbonate of soda
50g sugar or sweetener
50g agave syrup
115g vegetable oil
250ml soya yogurt
150g plums (stoned and chopped into small pieces)
A sprinkle of toasted chopped almonds
preparation:
Preparation
In a mixing bowl, add the flour, ground almonds, ginger, baking powder, bicarbonate of soda and xylitol and stir through. Add the agave syrup, vegetable oil and soya yoghurt and mix - but not to thoroughly because you don't want to overwork the mixture. Stir through the chopped plums. In a muffin tray, place 12 muffin cases and spoon the mixture to evenly fill the cases. Bake in the oven for 20-25 minutes until cooked through. Pop a skewer through one of the muffins and if it comes out clean, they're cooked. Method
Pre-heat the oven to 180C.

title: Quinoa Breakfast Bowl Porridge
ingredients:
50g (? cup or 1? oz approx) quinoa
200ml (? cup or 7fl oz approx) coconut milk
50ml (about a ? cup or 1? fl oz) water
5 pitted dates
Sprinkle of cinnamon (optional)
Fresh berries (optional)
preparation:
Preparation
Rinse quinoa thoroughly a using an extra fine sieve. This removes any of the natural soapy, saponins that sometimes coat quinoa.
Chop dates into small pieces and then add all ingredients to a pan (leaving out the cinnamon or berries, which will be added once served).
Bring pan to the boil and simmer on a low heat for 25 minutes (or until the quinoa has absorbed all of the liquid). Stir once in a while with a wooden spoon. The dates should slowly fall apart melting into the pan to create a delicious sweetness. If the dates are particularly tough, then encourage the breaking down process by pressing them down with your spoon.
Once cooked enjoy your quinoa bowl served hot or cold, adding a sprinkle of cinnamon and/or a small handful of fresh berries.

title: Fig and Date Smoothie
ingredients:
4 figs
5 dried dates
1/2 cup water
1/2 banana
1/2 cup soy yoghurt (yogurt)
1 tbsp. Pulsin pea protein powder (or your favorite protein powder)
preparation:
Method
Wash the figs and remove the stems. Now, add all ingredients to your blender and blend until smooth. Enjoy!

title: Orange Goji Smoothie
ingredients:
1 orange, peeled and segmented
? frozen banana
1 ? tbsp goji berries
1 tbsp ground flaxseed or chia seeds
Juice from half a lime
70 ml (1/3 cup) chilled coconut water
2 tbsp of Pulsin protein powder (pea or soya bean work well) (or 25g silken tofu or squash or 10g of cashews)
5 ice cubes
OPTIONAL INGREDIENTS
1 tsp baobab superfruit powder
1 tsp camu berry powder
Goji berries (to garnish)
preparation:
Method
Throw all the ingredients in a blender. Whizz until smooth. Taste and adjust if required. This smoothie is sweet enough for my palate, but it you want it sweeter, you can add ? tsp agave or date syrup or a few drops of stevia. Pour into a glass. Decorate with a few goji berries and serve. Enjoy!
